pattern: Computing each node's subtree sum from its children's sums in one post-order pass lets a frequency tally pick the most common total.
hints: The sum of a subtree equals the node's own value plus the subtree sums of its two children.
Process children before their parent so each subtree sum is ready when you reach the parent, and tally how often each sum occurs.
After collecting all subtree sums, return every sum that hits the maximum frequency.

edge_inputs.description: three distinct subtree sums each occur once
children cancel so the root sum repeats a child sum
negative values produce a unique most-frequent total
tie between equal subtree sums on both sides

stress.description: random level-order tree with holes and a small value range to force ties
stress.note: narrow value range encourages frequency ties to exercise the tie branch
